overview.tagline: One of New Jersey's few remaining villages, with small-town heart and big-city access
overview.description: Ridgefield Park is one of only four remaining villages in New Jersey, a distinction that reflects its strong sense of community identity. Located in southern Bergen County along the Hackensack River and Overpeck Creek, the village offers affordable housing, a diverse population, and convenient access to New York City. Ridgefield Park maintains its own police department, fire department, and school district while fostering a close-knit community atmosphere with tree-lined streets and active civic organizations.
highlights: One of only four villages remaining in New Jersey
Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Tree-lined streets with a strong neighborhood feel
Annual July 4th parade — one of the longest-running in the nation
Convenient access to NJ Transit bus service to NYC

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. The village features a mix of single-family homes, two-family homes, and smaller apartment buildings.
realEstate.recentTrends: Ridgefield Park has seen increased buyer interest due to its affordability relative to surrounding Bergen County communities. First-time buyers and young families have been drawn to the village. Home prices have appreciated moderately, and the two-family home market provides income potential for owner-occupants.
neighborhoodNotes: Main Street area — Center of village life with shops and services
The Heights — Elevated section with single-family homes and river views
Eastern section — Near Overpeck Park and the Hackensack River
Western section — Residential streets near the Bogota border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Village of Ridgefield Park Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on village website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling collected weekly by the Village.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Village
Yard waste collected seasonally from spring through fall
Recycling drop-off available at DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Village.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Village DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Sewer charges included on property tax bills
Emergency water repairs available through Veolia 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Village Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new work

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Village regulates street parking and operates municipal lots.
details: Municipal parking lots available in the downtown area
Street parking subject to posted regulations
Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning
Overnight parking restrictions on village streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced

additionalServices.title: Ridgefield Park Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 107 Cedar Street
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Community meeting room available for public use
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for village residents.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs at the Community Center
Community pool at Veterans Park
Annual village events and celebrations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Ridgefield Park enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must comply with village height regulations
Property owners must maintain curbs and sidewalks abutting their property
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dog licensing and leash laws are enforced throughout the village.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or dangerous animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the village.
keyRules: No overnight parking on village streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning
Snow emergency bans require vehicles off-street
Commercial vehicles over a certain weight prohibited on residential streets overnight
Parking meters enforced in commercial zones

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and inspected.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Village
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy
Occupancy limits enforced based on unit size

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Ridgefield Park protects its tree canopy through ordinances regulating tree removal.
keyRules: Permit required to remove significant trees on private property
Village Shade Tree Commission oversees public tree management
Replacement planting may be required when trees are removed
Damage to public trees subject to fines

publicSchools.name: Lincoln Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving early elementary grades

publicSchools.name: Ridgefield Park Jr./Sr. High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior and senior high school known as the Scarlets

privateSchools.name: St. Francis Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving the village and surrounding communities

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey City University (Jersey City) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: One of the most affordable communities in Bergen County
Strong small-town feel as one of NJ's few remaining villages
Convenient bus service to Manhattan via NJ Transit
Diverse, welcoming community with active civic life
Near Overpeck County Park for outdoor recreation

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Multiple NJ Transit bus routes provide service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. The village does not have a train station, but bus service is frequent and direct. The George Washington Bridge and Lincoln Tunnel are accessible via local highways.

costOfLiving.summary: Ridgefield Park offers one of the most affordable cost of living options in Bergen County, making it attractive to first-time homebuyers and families on a budget.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 5% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Very affordable by Bergen County standards
Strong community identity and village governance
Annual July 4th parade tradition dating back over 100 years
cons: No train station within the village
Schools rated below Bergen County average
Some flood-prone areas near the Hackensack River
Higher property tax rate compared to some neighboring towns

restaurants.name: Ridgefield Park Pizzeria
restaurants.description: Classic neighborhood pizzeria serving thin-crust pizza, heroes, and Italian-American favorites for decades.

restaurants.name: El Unico Restaurant
restaurants.description: Peruvian and Latin American restaurant serving authentic dishes including ceviche, lomo saltado, and rotisserie chicken.

restaurants.name: Top's Diner
restaurants.description: Iconic New Jersey diner consistently rated among the best diners in the state, famous for its extensive menu and classic ambiance.

restaurants.name: Mekong Restaurant
restaurants.description: Vietnamese and Thai restaurant serving pho, pad Thai, and other Southeast Asian favorites.

parks.name: Overpeck County Park
parks.description: Large Bergen County park along the Overpeck Creek featuring walking and biking trails, sports fields, picnic areas, and the Overpeck Golf Course.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Village park with a community pool, sports fields, playground, and memorial honoring local veterans.

parks.name: George Washington Bridge County Park (nearby)
parks.description: Scenic park at the eastern end of Bergen County offering walking paths and views of the Hudson River and George Washington Bridge.

events.name: Ridgefield Park 4th of July Parade
events.description: One of the oldest and longest-running Independence Day parades in the United States, a beloved village tradition since the early 1900s.

events.name: Ridgefield Park Community Day
events.description: Annual village celebration with food, entertainment, games, and activities for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, and community festivities at Village Hall.

attractions.name: Overpeck County Park
attractions.description: Expansive county park offering outdoor recreation, nature trails, sports facilities, and scenic waterway views along Overpeck Creek.

attractions.name: Historic Village Downtown
attractions.description: The village's Main Street retains a traditional small-town character with local shops and services reflecting the community's diverse population.

shoppingAreas: Main Street — Village center with local shops and services
Bergen Town Center (Paramus) — Regional shopping center nearby

government.governingBody: Board of Commissioners (Walsh Act Commission form)

council.name: Jerry Dietz
council.title: Commissioner of Streets and Public Improvements

council.name: Robert Faziola
council.title: Commissioner of Parks and Public Property

government.meetingSchedule: Board of Commissioners meets on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Village Hall, 234 Main Street, Ridgefield Park, NJ 07660

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
